name: 118 Kovenant
description:
118 Kovenant is a welcoming Filipino cafe and restaurant located in Pooraka, serving homestyle Filipino meals to Adelaide’s northern suburbs. The venue brings together authentic culinary traditions and friendly local service, making it a popular dining spot for families and food enthusiasts.

Diners regularly highlight signature specialities such as rich kare-kare with crispy pork, sizzling pork sisig, grilled bangus, and traditional silog breakfast rice bowls paired with longganisa or cured beef tapa. The menu also features popular sharing platters, crispy chicken skin, and traditional desserts including halo-halo and ube treats. Alongside its hearty food selection, the cafe offers coffee, specialty drinks, and cocktails.

The restaurant accommodates dine-in, takeaway, and kerbside pickup, operating for lunch and dinner from Tuesday through Sunday. With a relaxed atmosphere and approachable staff, 118 Kovenant remains a cherished hub for Adelaide’s Filipino community and locals seeking genuine Southeast Asian flavours.

name: AGA'S STORE (Philippine Food Products Etc.)
description:
AGA'S STORE is a friendly Filipino grocery store situated on Commercial Road in Salisbury, Adelaide. Located within the Rainbow Cleaning System complex, this neighbourhood shop specialises in a diverse range of authentic Philippine ingredients, pantry staples, and specialty items.

Run by J-Mark and his family, the store makes clever use of its compact space to stock a wide array of popular Filipino products. Shoppers can explore an extensive selection of frozen goods, including milkfish and tilapia, alongside instant noodles, seasonings, savoury snacks, sweet treats, and everyday household cosmetics. Customers frequently appreciate the helpful customer service and the store's dedication to providing hard-to-find Pinoy favourites in the northern suburbs of Adelaide.

Whether you are looking to stock up on essential cooking supplies or discover classic Filipino treats, AGA'S STORE serves as a welcoming community hub for local residents. The store is open Wednesday through Sunday from 10:00 am to 5:00 pm, offering convenient access for weekend and weekday shopping.

name: AYVA Filipino Food and Grocery
description:
AYVA Filipino Food and Grocery is a vibrant shop and dine-in eatery on Pimpala Road in Old Reynella, serving the southern Adelaide community. Combining an authentic Asian supermarket with a welcoming local restaurant, it specialises in imported Filipino ingredients, pantry staples, and freshly prepared hot meals.

Locals praise the venue for its clean, well-presented premises and friendly, attentive team who ensure every visitor feels right at home. The eatery menu features popular Filipino comfort foods, including freshly grilled Filipino BBQ and specialty treats like chicken isaw chips. Visitors also appreciate the extensive selection of imported groceries, quality pantry products, and convenient takeaway options for quick family lunches.

Whether you are shopping for authentic regional ingredients, enjoying a catch-up meal with friends, or picking up delicious Filipino dishes after work, AYVA provides a warm, authentic taste of the Philippines in Adelaide’s south.

name: Adelaide Filipino Ethnic School
description:
Adelaide Filipino Ethnic School (ADFES) is a vibrant community language institution dedicated to preserving and promoting Filipino language, culture, and heritage among young Filipino-Australians in South Australia. Operating under the umbrella of Community Language Schools SA (CLSSA), the school provides a supportive educational environment for students ranging from Reception to Year 9.

Classes are primarily held on Saturdays at 201 Port Road in Queenstown, where experienced educators lead interactive lessons in Tagalog. In addition to formal language training, the school organises engaging cultural workshops that immerse children in traditional Filipino arts, folk dance, and music. Parents warmly praise the school for nurturing dedicated teaching standards while inspiring a genuine sense of cultural pride and confidence in their children. Through annual community events and festival celebrations, ADFES plays a vital role in keeping Filipino traditions alive for the next generation in Adelaide.

name: Adelaide Filipino Lions Club
description:
The Adelaide Filipino Lions Club is the first Filipino Lions Club established in Australia, serving as an active non-profit community service organisation within Lions District 201C1. Dedicated to fostering civic pride and cultural connection, the club brings together Filipino-Australian volunteers and supporters across metropolitan Adelaide, South Australia.

The club actively engages in local community service projects, environmental initiatives such as the City of Playford's Adopt a Spot program at Smithfield Memorial Park, and broader humanitarian fundraising efforts. In addition to civic volunteer work, members host vibrant social and cultural gatherings, including Philippine Independence Day celebrations, community cabaret dinners, and inclusive wellness events like free Coffee and Zumba sessions.

Whether collaborating with neighbouring Lions clubs or organising grassroots community events, the Adelaide Filipino Lions Club provides a warm, welcoming environment for locals to build strong relationships and give back. Community members interested in getting involved or attending upcoming activities can connect with the club online or reach out directly to learn more.

name: Adelaide North Filipino Congregation
description:
Adelaide North Filipino Congregation is a welcoming Christian place of worship serving the Filipino-Australian community in Northgate, South Australia. Conveniently located on Folland Avenue in Adelaide's northern suburbs, the congregation offers a friendly and supportive environment for individuals and families seeking spiritual growth and community connection.

Meetings and Bible studies are conducted in Tagalog, making it easier for Tagalog speakers to participate fully in worship and connect with others in their native language. Attendees value the thoughtful discussions and practical guidance offered during services, noting that scriptural principles are explained clearly and applied to everyday modern life.

The congregation hosts regular gatherings focused on spiritual fellowship, prayer, and practical Christian living. The premises feature a wheelchair-accessible entrance and parking to ensure all visitors are comfortably accommodated. Whether you are a long-term local resident or newly arrived in South Australia, Adelaide North Filipino Congregation provides a warm, faith-centred community where members can build meaningful relationships and celebrate their shared heritage.

name: Adelaide SDA Filipino Fellowship
description:
The Adelaide SDA Filipino Fellowship is a vibrant community group connecting Filipino-Australian Seventh-day Adventists across South Australia. Serving as a warm spiritual home and cultural network, the fellowship brings families together to celebrate faith, heritage, and community fellowship in the greater Adelaide region.

A cornerstone of the fellowship's outreach is its musical ministry, notably featuring the acclaimed Christian singing group Tinig. Formed in 2019, Tinig—meaning 'voices' in Filipino—performs across South Australia to share spiritual messages through music, bringing inspiration and comfort to local congregations and community events.

Beyond musical performances, the Adelaide SDA Filipino Fellowship fosters connection through regular gatherings, spiritual encouragement, and cultural support for new arrivals and long-time residents alike. The fellowship actively collaborates with local Adventist churches across Adelaide, offering an inclusive environment where members can express their faith while preserving Filipino traditions and hospitality.

name: Adelaide University Filipino Club
description:
The Adelaide University Filipino Club (AUFC) is an official student organisation affiliated with YouX (formerly the Adelaide University Student Association) at the University of Adelaide. Established to support both domestic and international students, the club creates an inclusive cultural hub where members can connect, make friends, and celebrate vibrant Filipino heritage.

AUFC organises a diverse program of social gatherings and cultural initiatives throughout the academic year. Key highlights include welcome nights, karaoke sessions, sports competitions such as dodgeball and badminton, trivia nights, and bake sales featuring favourite traditional treats. The organisation also coordinates joint events with interstate Filipino student groups, fostering a broad network of support for students across Australia.

Membership is open to all university students who want to experience Filipino hospitality, language, and culture. Whether attending a cultural fiesta or joining regular campus meetups, members enjoy a welcoming environment designed to ease the transition into university life and enrich the student experience in Adelaide.

name: Asian Aussie Street Food
description:
Asian Aussie Street Food is a vibrant mobile food truck and catering business based in Christies Beach, South Australia. The vendor specialises in a modern fusion of traditional Filipino flavours and classic street food favourites, bringing authentic Pinoy dishes directly to local communities across Adelaide.

Popular menu offerings include tender, smoky pork barbecue skewers, flavorful pork and chicken burritos, crispy spring rolls, juicy burgers, loaded chips, and traditional silog meals. Customer feedback consistently highlights the generous portion sizes, high quality of ingredients, and warm, attentive service provided at major local events, food festivals, and private catering functions.

Operating out of Christies Beach, Asian Aussie Street Food frequently trades at Adelaide markets, community gatherings, and regional shows, as well as providing corporate and private party catering. It serves as a beloved culinary bridge for both the local Filipino community and food enthusiasts seeking authentic Asian fusion street food in South Australia.

name: Bamboo House
description:
Located in Ingle Farm Plaza in Adelaide's northern suburbs, Bamboo House is a cherished local spot serving traditional, home-style Filipino cuisine and Asian groceries. Known for its welcoming 'lutong bahay' feel, the eatery offers a warm and casual atmosphere for locals seeking authentic Pinoy flavours.

The menu features popular classic dishes such as Dinuguan, Bopis, Caldereta, Laing, and Sizzling Sisig. Diners also enjoy specialty items like chicken satay skewers, sweet chilli chicken, squid balls, and refreshing bubble tea. Beyond prepared meals, the shop stocks a selection of imported Filipino groceries, snacks, and kakanin, making it a convenient one-stop destination for both dining and pantry supplies.

With options for dine-in, quick takeaway, and local delivery, Bamboo House accommodates everyday family meals as well as community gatherings. The team also supports local events, catering for special occasions, cargo services to the Philippines, and worldwide money transfers.

name: Budok's Filipino Kitchen
description:
Budok's Filipino Kitchen is a popular eatery situated inside Chinatown Plaza on Moonta Street in Adelaide's vibrant central business district. The kitchen specialises in authentic Filipino dishes, offering a welcoming food-court dining experience alongside convenient takeaway options for local workers and visitors alike.

Diners frequently praise the generous portion sizes and rich, comforting flavours featured across the menu. Signature specialties include crispy lechon belly, slow-cooked balbacua, and charcoal-stuffed grilled squid. Regular visitors particularly favour the crowd-pleasing crispy chicken skin, seasoned grilled milkfish, and hearty pork sisig served with rice. For larger gatherings, the kitchen also provides traditional Boodle Fight feasts designed to share among family and friends.

Open seven days a week with extended evening trading on Fridays and Saturdays, Budok's Filipino Kitchen serves as an accessible dining destination for anyone looking to enjoy traditional Pinoy home-style cooking in the heart of Adelaide.

name: C&O Pinoy Goods
description:
C&O Pinoy Goods was a dedicated local Filipino grocery store situated along Churchill Road in Kilburn, South Australia. Conveniently positioned to serve residents and families across northern Adelaide, the shop specialised in providing a wide selection of imported Filipino food items, pantry staples, and everyday household products directly to the local community.

The store offered a comprehensive variety of essential cooking ingredients required for authentic home-cooked meals, including regional soy sauces, native vinegars, bagoong, dried herbs, and specialty seasoning mixes for classic dishes such as adobo, sinigang, and menudo. Visitors could also browse through shelves stocked with popular Filipino snacks, biscuits, instant noodle varieties, frozen products, and traditional sweet delicacies.

Beyond its role as a neighbourhood retail store, C&O Pinoy Goods served as a familiar and welcoming hub for the Filipino-Australian community seeking trusted grocery brands and comforting flavours from the Philippines within the greater Adelaide metropolitan area.

This business has permanently closed.

name: Cebu Soul
description:
Cebu Soul is a popular Filipino food truck and mobile catering service based in Adelaide, South Australia. Helmed by Chef Juve, the food truck specialises in authentic Cebuano island street food and traditional grilled dishes, bringing vibrant Philippine flavours to festivals, corporate functions, and private events across metropolitan Adelaide.

The menu highlights charcoal-grilled pork and chicken BBQ skewers, hearty Silog rice bowls served with pickled radish and Asian greens, pork adobo humba, and pancit guisado. Customers can also enjoy popular street food snacks such as loaded BBQ fries, crispy vegetable spring rolls, and fried banana turon for dessert.

Visitors to major South Australian events, including the Adelaide Fringe, The Garden of Unearthly Delights, and regional food festivals, frequently seek out Cebu Soul for its friendly staff and authentic charcoal-grilled dishes. Whether catering a private party or serving crowds at a local street market, Cebu Soul offers an enjoyable taste of Cebuano food culture in South Australia.

name: Chenvel by Kaizen Adelaide
description:
Chenvel by Kaizen is a reliable freight forwarding and cargo transport service providing door-to-door Balikbayan box shipping from Adelaide and South Australia to destinations across the Philippines. Specialising in personal cargo transport and sea freight, the service connects local Filipino-Australian families with loved ones back home.

Customers consistently praise the company for its seamless, stress-free booking and pickup process. Feedback highlights the helpfulness of the team during collection, reasonable pricing, and the strong, durable boxes that ensure goods arrive safely and intact. Clients also appreciate convenient options such as local mobile pickup in Adelaide as well as discounted self-drop-off options for multi-box shipments.

Whether sending gifts, personal items, or holiday packages, Chenvel by Kaizen offers clear transit tracking and dependable handling for Luzon, Visayas, and Mindanao. With dedicated customer support and scheduled sea freight runs, it remains a trusted logistics partner for the Adelaide Filipino community.

name: Couples for Christ Adelaide
description:
Couples for Christ (CFC) Adelaide is a vibrant lay Catholic community movement dedicated to renewing and strengthening Christian family life across South Australia. Established locally in September 1992, the organisation supports hundreds of families and active members throughout Adelaide and regional South Australia, serving parish communities from Bordertown to Port Wakefield.

The cornerstone of the movement is the Christian Life Program (CLP), an integrated formation course designed to help couples deepen their relationship with God and build supportive community connections. Beyond couples' formation, CFC Adelaide provides tailored family ministries for all life stages, including CFC Kids for Christ, Youth for Christ, Singles for Christ, Handmaids of the Lord, and Servants of the Lord.

Members value the warm, faith-filled environment and practical encouragement that help families navigate modern challenges together. Through regular household meetings, spiritual retreats, and parish outreach, Couples for Christ Adelaide fosters a supportive network centred on Christian values, fellowship, and active service in the local Catholic community.

name: D&J Filipino Store
description:
D&J Filipino Store is a specialist retail shop located on Hanson Road in Athol Park, Adelaide. It serves as a practical community hub for Filipino-Australians living across South Australia, offering imported goods and essential logistics services.

Local community members frequently visit the store to purchase Balikbayan boxes and arrange international shipping to the Philippines. The shop provides access to trusted cargo and remittance partners, including Chanvel and LBC, making it a convenient one-stop destination for sending care packages and gifts to loved ones back home.

In addition to freight services, D&J Filipino Store supplies a range of popular Pinoy pantry staples, specialty ingredients, snacks, and everyday household items tailored to the needs of the local community in Adelaide's western suburbs.

Whether you are preparing a care package or looking for familiar flavours from home, D&J Filipino Store has long supported the local diaspora. Please note that this venue is currently listed as temporarily closed.

name: Dea's Kitchen & Pinoy Delicacies
description:
Dea's Kitchen & Pinoy Delicacies is a family-operated online shop and food distributor based in Greenacres, Adelaide. Dedicated to bringing a genuine taste of home to the Filipino community across South Australia and nationwide, the business specialises in imported sweets, nostalgic childhood treats, and popular provincial delicacies from the Philippines.

Customers can browse an extensive selection of classic snacks, regional specialties, and iconic Filipino food brands. Alongside standard grocery items, Dea's Kitchen offers customised Pasabuy and Pasabox personal shopping services, helping locals source coveted products and seasonal pre-orders like Dunkin' Donuts munchkins directly from the Philippines.

Operating through their online storefront, Dea's Kitchen dispatches orders efficiently via Australia Post from their Adelaide hub, while also catering to local orders. Whether you are craving favourite childhood sweets or seeking hard-to-find Filipino pantry staples, Dea's Kitchen & Pinoy Delicacies provides a convenient and reliable connection to home for Filipinos living in Adelaide and across Australia.

name: Ditobi - Filipino Restaurant
description:
Ditobi - Filipino Restaurant is a vibrant, family-owned dining venue situated on Grote Street in the heart of Adelaide CBD. Known for its warm atmosphere and authentic flavours, the restaurant specialises in traditional Filipino dishes crafted with care and family tradition.

Locals and visitors praise the generous portion sizes and comforting meals. Popular offerings include crisp pork sisig served on a sizzling plate, rich pork kare-kare, and savoury palabok. Guests also enjoy hearty beef pares, pinoy pork belly skewers, lumpia, and classic silogs served with garlic rice. For dessert, house specialties include rich ube cake, ensaymada pastries, and refreshing ube and mango shakes.

The welcoming dining room provides a cosy space for casual gatherings, family meals, and celebratory dinners. With convenient parking nearby and a location close to city transit, Ditobi is a favoured spot for both the local Filipino community and food enthusiasts exploring authentic Southeast Asian cuisine in South Australia. Reservations are recommended on weekends.

name: Dr. Angeline Roque - InHealth Medical West Lakes
description:
Dr. Angeline Roque (also practicing as Dr. Angeline Roxas-Roque) is a bilingual Filipino General Practitioner (GP) based at InHealth Medical West Lakes in Adelaide. Holding a Doctor of Medicine and Fellowship with the Royal Australian College of General Practitioners (FRACGP), Dr. Roque brings extensive clinical expertise and a compassionate approach to patient care.

Her key areas of clinical interest encompass Women's Health, Family Medicine, and Paediatrics. In addition to primary general practice, Dr. Roque possesses specialised experience in dermatology, skin cancer checks, and aesthetic skin treatments. Patients consistently praise her gentle demeanour, warm approachability, and thorough care during consultations.

Fluent in both English and Filipino-Tagalog, Dr. Roque provides comprehensive, culturally sensitive medical services to individuals and families across Adelaide's western suburbs, including West Lakes, Tennyson, Grange, Semaphore Park, and Seaton.

Located within the modern InHealth Medical Centre in the WEST development on Charles Street, West Lakes, the clinic operates Monday to Friday from 8:30 am to 5:00 pm, featuring on-site general practice, allied health, and pathology services.

name: Dr. Cristina Bolaton - Fountain Valley Medical Centre
description:
Dr Cristina Bolaton is a bilingual Filipino General Practitioner (GP) offering comprehensive healthcare at Fountain Valley Medical Centre in Happy Valley, Adelaide. She earned her Doctor of Medicine from Saint Louis University in the Philippines in 1992 and attained her Fellowship of the Royal Australian College of General Practitioners (FRACGP) in 2011.

Fluent in English, Tagalog, and Ilocano, Dr Bolaton specialises in family medicine, women's health, antenatal care, child immunisations, chronic disease management, and preventative care. Patients value her calm, welcoming, and conscientious manner, praising her commitment to listening attentively and providing individualised medical treatment.

Beyond her general practice work, Dr Bolaton is an active contributor to the local Filipino-Australian community as a long-standing member of Adelaide's Sandiwa Choir. Situated inside the Valley Central Shopping Centre on Kenihans Road, Fountain Valley Medical Centre provides modern, accessible medical services for patients across Happy Valley and nearby southern Adelaide communities.

name: Dr. Melanie Mateo - Health on Henley
description:
Dr. Melanie Mateo is a dedicated, bilingual Filipino General Practitioner (GP) providing comprehensive medical care at Health on Henley in Brooklyn Park, Adelaide. Having earned her medical degree from the University of Santo Tomas in the Philippines, Dr. Mateo is a Fellow of the Royal Australian College of General Practitioners (FRACGP). She specialises in family medicine, women's health, paediatrics, and chronic condition management, catering to patients of all ages.

Patients frequently praise Dr. Mateo and her team for their warm, attentive care, thorough medical investigations, and clear communication. Rather than offering rushed consultations, the clinic focuses on building lasting doctor-patient relationships. The modern practice provides accessible healthcare options, including routine check-ups, immunisations, and specialised services such as microsuction ear cleaning.

Fluent in English, Tagalog (Filipino), and Dutch, Dr. Mateo offers a welcoming healthcare home for both the local Adelaide community and Filipino families seeking culturally responsive medical advice. Health on Henley operates Monday through Friday on Henley Beach Road, featuring convenient online booking and on-site parking.

name: Dr. Susan Ravida - Adelaide Smile Centre
description:
Dr. Susan Ravida is a dedicated Filipino dentist practicing at Adelaide Smile Centre (operating as Bupa Dental Grange Road) in Findon, Adelaide. After graduating with a Doctor of Dental Medicine from the University of the Philippines in 1997, Dr. Ravida has provided compassionate oral health care across South Australia since 2008. She brings extensive postgraduate expertise in paediatric dentistry, aesthetic procedures, and preventative care.

Patients consistently commend the clinic’s warm, patient-first approach and reassuring environment. Reviews praise the team's clear explanations, gentle check-ups, thorough hygiene cleans, and supportive care for anxious patients and young children. The Findon clinic delivers a complete range of family dental services, including routine examinations, professional cleans, cosmetic treatments, veneers, and emergency dental appointments.

Situated conveniently on Grange Road in Adelaide's western suburbs, the practice offers wheelchair-accessible facilities and extended weekday opening hours. Dr. Ravida speaks both English and Tagalog fluently, providing accessible, personalised dental care to Filipino families and the wider local community.

name: Dr. Welyn Gordo - Dental Excellence
description:
Dr Welyn Gordo is an experienced Filipino dentist practising at Dental Excellence, situated on Pirie Street in the heart of the Adelaide CBD. After earning her dentistry degree from the University of the East in 1997 and completing her Australian Dental Council qualifications, Dr Gordo has delivered comprehensive oral health care across South Australia for over two decades.

At Dental Excellence, Dr Gordo provides a complete suite of services including general check-ups, preventative care, wisdom teeth removal, oral surgery, and modern orthodontic treatments. The practice features advanced in-house diagnostic tools such as digital X-rays, intra-oral cameras, and laser-assisted periodontal treatments, allowing patients to undergo thorough care in a modern environment.

Patients frequently praise the team’s gentle, thorough approach and transparent communication. The clinic is well regarded for putting anxious patients at ease through clear treatment explanations and attentive, personalised care. Located just one block from Rundle Mall, Dental Excellence offers convenient weekday appointments for families, students, and workers seeking quality dental care in Adelaide.

name: Dr. Welyn Gordo - Personal Dental Care
description:
Dr Welyn Gordo is an experienced Filipino dentist practising at Personal Dental Care in Hillcrest, Adelaide. She graduated with a degree in Dentistry in 1997 and completed her Australian Dental Council certification after moving to Australia. With over two decades of clinical experience, Dr Gordo provides comprehensive family dental care for patients of all ages.

Her practice covers general dental check-ups, preventative treatments, restorative procedures, oral surgery including wisdom teeth extractions, and orthodontic options. Patients value her gentle approach, thorough examinations, and focus on creating a supportive environment, especially for anxious patients or families seeking long-term oral health care. Personal Dental Care operates from a modern, accessible clinic with welcoming nursing and reception staff.

Situated at 501 North East Road in Hillcrest, the practice serves residents across the northeastern suburbs of Adelaide, including Gilles Plains and neighbouring areas. The clinic is open Monday to Saturday, providing convenient evening appointments on Tuesdays and Thursdays for local families.

name: Dulcelucks
description:
Dulcelucks is a popular local Filipino home dessert shop and takeaway bakery situated on Wright Road in Valley View, Adelaide. Established by Dulce Menor, this neighbourhood bakery has built a strong reputation across Adelaide's northern suburbs for its authentic selection of traditional Filipino sweets, handcrafted treats, and custom celebration cakes.

Local patrons frequently praise the shop's signature halo-halo, highlighting its refreshing composition that achieves a balanced flavour without relying excessively on condensed milk or heavy syrups. Their custom ube cakes remain a favourite choice for local birthday celebrations and family gatherings. Beyond custom cakes and halo-halo, Dulcelucks offers a wide variety of freshly baked homemade Filipino delicacies crafted for convenient takeaway pickup.

As a dedicated family-run bakery in Valley View, Dulcelucks provides an accessible and welcoming destination for anyone eager to experience genuine Filipino baking traditions in South Australia. Customers can easily arrange custom cake orders or place inquiries by contacting the bakery directly at 0487 388 931, or by connecting through their active Facebook and Instagram profiles.

name: Faba Sports
description:
The Filipino-Australian Basketball Association (FABA Sports) is a vibrant community sporting organisation based in Adelaide, South Australia. Established to bring people together through a shared love of basketball, FABA Sports serves as an active hub for the local Filipino-Australian community, promoting fitness, teamwork, and warm camaraderie.

FABA Sports organises competitive basketball leagues and tournaments across multiple divisions, catering to players of all ages and experience levels. Offerings include junior development competitions, senior divisions, and over-45 leagues. The association runs structured Summer and Winter seasons, bringing together local teams, families, and supporters for spirited weekend matches across Adelaide basketball courts.

Beyond the basketball court, FABA Sports focuses on fostering strong community connections and cultural pride. It offers a welcoming environment where new residents and sports enthusiasts can build lifelong friendships. Whether you are looking to join a team, coach, or cheer from the grandstands, FABA Sports welcomes players and families across metropolitan Adelaide.

name: Filipino Association of South Australia Inc
description:
The Filipino Association of South Australia Inc. (Fil-ASA) is a cornerstone community organisation in Adelaide, established in 1975 and incorporated in 1978. Serving as a welcoming network for Filipino migrants across South Australia, Fil-ASA celebrates over five decades of dedicated service to the local community.

The association brings together Filipino-Australians and the wider community through cultural preservation, social gatherings, educational initiatives, and welfare support. Over the years, Fil-ASA has spearheaded numerous initiatives, including traditional cultural celebrations, community radio broadcasting, and dedicated support groups for elderly community members. Its mission centres on uniting Filipinos, fostering mutual understanding, and offering practical assistance to help newcomers settle into South Australian life.

Whether you are looking to connect with fellow community members, participate in cultural events, or seek support, Fil-ASA provides an active network and warm sense of belonging. The association continues to host regular events and milestone celebrations across the greater Adelaide region.

name: Filipino Australian Basketball Association (FABA Sports Adelaide)
description:
The Filipino Australian Basketball Association (FABA Sports Adelaide) is a vibrant sports organisation based in Mawson Lakes, South Australia. Established to bring the local Filipino-Australian community together through a shared passion for basketball, FABA Sports runs inclusive competitions for players of all ages and skill levels.

The association organises regular summer and winter seasons featuring junior, senior, and Over-45 divisions. Matches take place across local indoor courts on Sundays, where competitive games are paired with a warm, family-friendly atmosphere. Off the court, sideline gatherings often feature traditional Filipino barbecue and community social events, making match days feel like a true cultural celebration.

Whether you want to join an established team as a player, register a new team, or support local athletes from the stands, FABA Sports offers a welcoming environment where sportsmanship, culture, and lifelong friendships collide. You can follow their season fixtures, ladders, and player statistics on their official website or connect with the league coordinators directly via email.

name: Filipino Bakehaus & Asian Groceries
description:
Filipino Bakehaus & Asian Groceries was a cherished community store situated along Commercial Road in Port Adelaide, South Australia. Serving the broader Adelaide metropolitan area, the shop specialised in authentic Filipino baked treats alongside an extensive selection of imported groceries, traditional pantry staples, and regional Asian food items.

Customers appreciated the well-kept environment and tidy layout of the venue, which allowed visitors to easily browse through specialty cooking sauces, rice varieties, frozen goods, and popular Filipino snacks despite the cozy footprint. From daily essential ingredients to favourite childhood sweets, the business provided a convenient taste of home for local Filipino-Australian families and Asian culinary enthusiasts alike.

Beyond its retail shelves, the establishment offered a welcoming touch of Filipino hospitality in the western suburbs, making it a popular stop for residents seeking hard-to-find ingredients and traditional pantry supplies.

This business has permanently closed.

name: Filipino Catholic Chaplaincy Adelaide
description:
The Filipino Catholic Chaplaincy Adelaide provides pastoral care, chaplaincy services, and multicultural Masses for the local Filipino community across metropolitan Adelaide. Operating under the Catholic Archdiocese of Adelaide in partnership with the Filipino Catholic Pastoral Support Committee (FCPSC), the chaplaincy supports Filipino migrants and parishioners by nourishing faith and preserving cherished cultural traditions.

Regular monthly Tagalog and multicultural Masses are celebrated at several host churches across the city. Services take place at St Augustine's Church in Salisbury on the first Sunday of the month at 3:00 PM, St Luke's Church in Noarlunga Downs on the second Sunday at 2:00 PM, Mater Christi Church in Seaton on the third Sunday at 3:00 PM, and St Martin's Church in Greenacres on the fourth Sunday at 4:00 PM.

Beyond regular liturgical services, the community organises major cultural and religious celebrations, including traditional Simbang Gabi dawn Masses during Christmas and annual Santacruzan festivities. The chaplaincy offers a warm and welcoming space for families to connect, worship, and build strong community ties in South Australia.

name: Filipino Catholic Community - St Augustine's Salisbury
description:
The Filipino Catholic Community at St Augustine's Church provides a vibrant spiritual home and pastoral gathering place for Filipino families, students, and workers across Salisbury and Northern Adelaide. Nestled within the welcoming Salisbury Catholic Parish at 23 Commercial Road, this active community organises regular Tagalog and Filipino Mass services on the first Sunday of every month at 3:00 PM.

Community members and visitors appreciate the parish's warm, inclusive atmosphere, spacious church facilities, and lively musical accompaniment supported by dedicated local choirs. Worshippers often highlight the friendly environment where parishioners gather to celebrate special occasions and share fellowship. Beyond monthly religious services, the community fosters meaningful social connections through morning teas and pastoral activities, helping newcomers and long-term residents build lasting friendships.

Supported by the Catholic Archdiocese of Adelaide's chaplaincy network, the group serves as a vital cultural hub, preserving cherished Filipino faith traditions and language while actively enriching the diverse, multicultural life of the Salisbury parish.

name: Filipino Catholic Pastoral Support Committee
description:
The Filipino Catholic Pastoral Support Committee (FCPSC) is an essential community organisation operating under the Catholic Archdiocese of Adelaide. Dedicated to supporting the spiritual and social well-being of Filipino Catholics across South Australia, the committee provides warm pastoral care for both new arrivals and long-standing community members.

Working in close partnership with the Catholic Multicultural Office and dedicated chaplains, the committee organises monthly Tagalog and multicultural Masses across various parishes in the greater metropolitan area. Key gathering places include St Augustine's Church in Salisbury, St Luke's Church in Noarlunga Downs, Mater Christi Church in Seaton, and St Martin's Church in Greenacres.

Beyond regular worship, the committee brings traditional Filipino religious celebrations to life throughout the year. These include the vibrant feast of Sto. Niño de Cebu, the colourful Santacruzan procession, and the cherished Simbang Gabi novena Masses during the Christmas season.

Whether you are looking to attend a Tagalog Mass, connect with local families, or preserve traditional faith heritage, the committee serves as a welcoming hub for the Filipino Catholic community in Adelaide.

name: Filipino Ethnic School of SA Inc
description:
The Filipino Ethnic School of SA Inc is a non-profit community language school dedicated to preserving and celebrating Filipino culture, language, and heritage in Adelaide. Operating within the Community Language Schools SA (CLSSA) network, the school provides structured Tagalog language education for school-aged children from Reception through to Year 9.

Alongside core language studies, the school organises interactive cultural workshops where young Filipino-Australians can explore traditional arts, dance, music, and heritage. These programs empower youth by fostering identity, cultural pride, and strong community connections. Classes are typically conducted on Saturdays during school terms, bringing families together in a welcoming educational environment.

Whether you are looking to enrol your children in Tagalog lessons or seeking to connect with the local Filipino-Australian community, the Filipino Ethnic School of SA Inc offers an enriching environment rooted in culture and community spirit.

name: Filipino League of Students in Flinders
description:
The Filipino League of Students in Flinders (FLSF) is a vibrant cultural student organisation based at Flinders University in Adelaide, South Australia. Affiliated with the Flinders University Student Association (FUSA), the club brings together Filipino international students, domestic students, and anyone with an interest in Filipino culture.

FLSF focuses on building a welcoming home away from home for students across all Flinders campuses. The club regularly organises social gatherings, cultural celebrations, Orientation Week welcomes, and community bonding events such as their annual Kamustahan sessions. Their dedication to student welfare and engagement was recognised at the FUSA Club Awards, where an executive member received the Club Member of the Year award.

Through an active presence on campus and digital platforms like Rubric and Instagram, FLSF connects members with local support networks, peer mentoring, and fun cultural experiences in Adelaide. Whether you want to celebrate traditional Filipino holidays, meet new friends, or share your love for Pinoy food and culture, the Filipino League of Students in Flinders provides a warm, supportive community throughout your university journey.

name: Filipino Settlement Coordinating Council of SA Inc
description:
The Filipino Settlement Coordinating Council of South Australia Inc. (FSCCSA) is a prominent peak body and umbrella organisation representing various Filipino community groups and regional associations across South Australia. Operating from Hallett Cove, the council serves as a vital central hub for the vibrant Filipino-Australian community in Adelaide and broader regional areas.

Dedicated to fostering unity and cultural pride, FSCCSA plays an essential role in assisting new arrivals and families with settlement and welfare support. The organisation actively collaborates with local groups to organise major multicultural celebrations, such as the annual Philippine Fiesta of SA, while supporting initiatives in education, arts, health, and career development.

Through its extensive network of member associations, the council advocates for the needs of the Filipino community and promotes strong intergenerational ties. Whether providing practical guidance for settlement, encouraging cultural preservation, or bringing community members together for major events, FSCCSA remains a welcoming and supportive cornerstone for Filipinos making South Australia their home.

name: Filipino Student Society UniSA
description:
The Filipino Student Society UniSA (FSS UniSA) is the official student organisation for Filipino students and cultural enthusiasts at the University of South Australia in Adelaide. Affiliated with the University of South Australia Student Association (USASA), the society is dedicated to celebrating and sharing vibrant Filipino culture across UniSA campuses, including City West and Mawson Lakes.

The society brings together local and international students through a diverse calendar of social gatherings, cultural celebrations, and community events. Highlights of their student activities include welcoming lunches for international students, casual StreetBall basketball nights, film screenings, and lively campus fair stalls. These events provide a warm and supportive environment where students can build lasting friendships, share traditional food and music, and find a comforting home away from home while studying in South Australia.

Whether you are a Filipino student settling into university life in Adelaide or simply interested in discovering Filipino traditions and hospitality, the society welcomes all members of the UniSA community. Students can easily get involved and become official members through the USASA clubs portal or by connecting with the group on Instagram.

name: Filipino-Australian Basketball Association (FABA Sports)
description:
The Filipino-Australian Basketball Association, widely known as FABA Sports, is a vibrant community sporting organisation based in Mawson Lakes, South Australia. Established to bring people together through a shared passion for basketball, FABA Sports provides an inclusive platform for players of all ages and skill levels across metropolitan Adelaide.

The association runs structured seasonal basketball competitions, including Winter and Summer leagues hosted on Sundays. Divisions cater to junior players, adult open teams, and veteran 'Senioritos' in Over 45 categories. Teams such as Barako, Goodstart Learning, TI Fencing, and Jojo Lechon regularly compete in spirited matches that celebrate teamwork, athleticism, and cultural pride.

Members and families value FABA Sports for its warm, welcoming environment that feels like a home away from home. Beyond on-court competition, the club actively fosters community health, youth development, and social connection among Filipino-Australians and the broader South Australian sporting community. Whether you are looking to play competitive hoops or support local grassroots sport, FABA Sports offers a friendly and engaging sports hub.

name: Filipino-Australian Community Services Inc
description:
Filipino-Australian Community Services Inc. (FACSI) is a dedicated non-profit organisation supporting elderly members of the Filipino-Australian community in South Australia. Formerly known as Filipino Home & Community Services, the organisation is based at Enfield Plaza on Main North Road in Enfield.

FACSI offers essential aged care support and personal assistance designed to help senior community members maintain independence and well-being. Services include household assistance, respite care for carers, and transport for appointments and local errands. Beyond personal care, FACSI organises regular social gatherings and cultural functions. These events bring community members together, reducing social isolation and celebrating Filipino heritage.

Affiliated with the Filipino Settlement Coordinating Council of South Australia (FSCCSA), FACSI works closely with local families and volunteers to deliver culturally sensitive care. FACSI provides a welcoming hub for seniors in the northern suburbs of Adelaide. It offers valuable assistance for families seeking support for an elderly relative or connecting with community initiatives.

name: Filipinos iConnect SA Inc
description:
Filipinos iConnect SA Inc is a registered not-for-profit community organisation based at Northwestern Community Church on Port Road in Queenstown, Adelaide. Dedicated to empowering and supporting the local Filipino-Australian community, the organisation operates with the core mission of helping one another to build a stronger network.

The organisation provides a wide range of essential support initiatives for individuals and families across South Australia. Its key services include community support programs, cultural events, job placement assistance, domestic violence awareness, and mental health support resources. In addition, Filipinos iConnect SA Inc runs the Adelaide Filipino Ethnic School (ADFES), offering language and cultural education to help younger generations stay connected to their heritage.

Community members regularly gather for social connection through weekly iConnect Dinners on Tuesday evenings during school terms, alongside workshops and cultural celebrations. Whether you are looking to volunteer, connect with fellow community members, or access support services in Adelaide, Filipinos iConnect SA Inc provides a warm and welcoming environment for everyone.

name: Filo Cleano Services
description:
Filo Cleano Services is a dedicated professional cleaning provider operating across metropolitan Adelaide and surrounding South Australian regions. As a Social Traders certified social enterprise and member of the Social Enterprise Association of South Australia (SASEC), the business specialises in residential, commercial, office, Airbnb short-stay, and end-of-lease bond cleaning services. They also offer tailored NDIS and aged care household assistance to support families with reliable home care.

Clients consistently highlight the team's strong attention to detail, punctuality, and friendly communication. Rooms are left spotless and fresh, with thorough cleaning across kitchens, bathrooms, living areas, and shared commercial spaces. Their flexible scheduling, easy online booking system, and transparent service make arranging property care straightforward and stress-free.

Operating out of Hackney, Filo Cleano Services is fully insured and requires all staff members to hold current police checks for complete peace of mind. Whether you require routine home maintenance, after-hours office cleaning, or specialised strata property upkeep, Filo Cleano Services delivers dependable, high-quality solutions across South Australia.

name: Filo Halo AU
description:
Filo Halo AU is a popular Filipino dessert and street food business based in Parafield Gardens, Adelaide. Specialising in traditional Filipino shaved ice treats, the vendor is best known for its signature halo-halo—a layered dessert combining shaved ice, evaporated milk, sweet coconut strings (macapuno), nata de coco, kaong, caramelised banana, and jackfruit, crowned with smooth leche flan and rich ube ice cream.

Beyond halo-halo, Filo Halo AU offers a variety of beloved sweet treats and snacks, including ube macapuno, mango sago, bilo bilo, taho, leche flan, and crispy turon, alongside savoury chicken and pork lumpia. The business operates online order pickups from Parafield Gardens and frequently appears as a pop-up vendor across Adelaide's local food festivals and market events, such as the Adelaide Central Market Producer in Residence program, the Glenelg Ice Cream Festival, and the OzAsia Festival. Whether attending a local festival or ordering sweet treats for pickup, Filo Halo AU offers an authentic taste of Filipino dessert culture to the South Australian community.

name: Filo-Cleano Services
description:
Filo-Cleano Services is a dedicated commercial and residential cleaning provider based in Adelaide, South Australia. Operating as a certified social enterprise, the organisation delivers tailored cleaning solutions with a mission to support migrant employment opportunities across the Adelaide metropolitan region.

Local clients consistently highlight the team’s meticulous attention to detail, punctuality, and approachable communication style. Service offerings cover general domestic house cleaning, deep end-of-lease cleans, office maintenance, Airbnb property management, and specialised NDIS and aged care household support. Customers frequently commend the simple online booking platform, flexible scheduling, and high-quality results that leave residential and commercial spaces fresh, spotless, and well-organised.

Whether managing routine commercial maintenance in the Adelaide city centre or assisting families in surrounding suburbs, Filo-Cleano Services offers fully insured, professional property care tailored to individual schedules.

name: Forex Cargo Adelaide
description:
Forex Cargo Adelaide is a trusted Balikbayan box and freight forwarding service provider catering to the Filipino community in Adelaide and across South Australia. As part of an established national logistics network operating since the 1990s, the service specialises in dependable, flat-rate door-to-door sea cargo shipping and personal effects delivery from South Australia to destinations throughout the Philippines.

Senders appreciate the convenient options for ordering sturdy, high-grade Australian-made Balikbayan boxes directly to their doorstep, as well as organised home pick-ups. With regular weekly container shipping schedules, customers can send care packages, gifts, and essential items with peace of mind. The service includes full online tracking, allowing senders to monitor their shipment’s journey from dispatch to final recipient delivery.

Supported by a dedicated customer care team for South Australian enquiries, Forex Cargo provides a vital logistics connection for local families wishing to send love and support back home to the Philippines.

name: GMG Visa Solutions
description:
GMG Visa Solutions is a family-owned Australian migration agency based in Blakeview, South Australia. Founded by registered migration consultants German Peralta Glico (MARN: 1460902) and Geroselene Glico-Kovarskis (MARN: 2015041), the firm specialises in assisting individuals, families, and workers with navigating Australian immigration pathways.

Clients appreciate their transparent guidance, prompt communication, and educational consultation style. They provide comprehensive support across a wide range of visa categories, including permanent residency, partner and family visas, student visas, employer-sponsored schemes, and skilled migration streams. In addition to visa processing, the agency assists with citizenship applications, skills assessments, health requirements, and complex visa appeals or cancellations.

Operating from Blakeview in northern Adelaide, GMG Visa Solutions serves both local residents and international clients moving to Australia. Their team takes time to understand individual personal circumstances, ensuring clients receive practical advice and clear expectations at every stage of their migration journey.

name: Gabito Lawyers
description:
Gabito Lawyers is a respected legal and migration practice based in Innovation House, Mawson Lakes, Adelaide. Principal Solicitor Cyril Gabito leads the firm in providing professional legal counsel to individuals, families, and businesses across South Australia.

The practice specialises in Australian immigration law, assisting clients with partner visa applications, work visas, visitor visas, and complex visa cancellation matters. Clients frequently commend the team for their deep legal knowledge, prompt communication, and supportive guidance throughout the migration process. In addition to migration services, the firm offers assistance with family law, wills and estates, and commercial matters.

Situated conveniently in Mawson Lakes, Gabito Lawyers serves as a trusted resource for the local Filipino-Australian community, helping clients navigate the intricacies of the Australian legal system with clarity and care. The office is open Monday to Friday from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM.

name: HENLEY SUMMER CLUB
description:
Henley Summer Club is a vibrant beachside pop-up cafe and community venue located along Seaview Road in Henley Beach, Adelaide. Operating as a welcoming seaside hangout spot, the cafe is widely celebrated for its relaxed coastal atmosphere, friendly service, and inventive food and drink selection.

Visitors frequently pop in for authentic Japanese matcha beverages, specialty coffee, refreshing smoothies, and handcrafted Asian-style shaved ice desserts such as bingsu in honey and honeydew flavours. A standout feature of the venue is its dynamic schedule of rotating kitchen takeovers. These regular culinary collaborations introduce diverse food pop-ups to the Henley Beach foreshore, featuring authentic Filipino ihaw-ihaw charcoal barbecue and savoury street food hosted by local caterers like Sugbahan alongside sweet treats from Kaya Desserts.

Situated directly opposite the beach in Adelaide's western suburbs, Henley Summer Club offers local residents and coastal visitors an inviting space to gather over morning coffee, afternoon sweets, or weekend evening meals by the ocean.

name: Honey's Cake House
description:
Honey's Cake House is a charming Filipino-owned cake shop and cafe located on Payneham Road in Evandale, Adelaide. Stepping inside reveals a lovely, Parisian-inspired atmosphere complete with cheerful pink decor, comfortable seating, and elegant chandeliers.

The bakery specialises in authentic Filipino celebration cakes and gourmet treats. Customers love the authentic flavour of their ube cake and ube cheesecake, which capture the traditional taste of the Philippines. Their menu also features popular options such as rich chocolate cake, fluffy pandan cake, traditional mamon, and vanilla custard flan cakes. Servings are available both as whole custom cakes for birthdays and milestones, or as individual slices to enjoy alongside coffee and specialty drinks.

Honey's Cake House is open Tuesday through Saturday from 1:00 PM until 8:00 PM, providing a welcoming afternoon and early evening spot for locals to gather and enjoy sweet treats. Dine-in and takeaway options are both available.

name: Iglesia Ni Cristo [Victoria] - Adelaide
description:
Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church of Christ) Local Congregation of Adelaide is a welcoming Christian fellowship serving the Filipino-Australian community in Magill and the broader Adelaide region. Situated on The Parade in Magill, the congregation offers regular worship services conducted in English to support both local families and newcomers settling in South Australia.

Congregants appreciate the warm, inclusive atmosphere and the dedicated spiritual guidance provided during weekly gatherings. Regular worship services take place on Wednesday evenings at 7:30 PM, Saturday evenings at 6:00 PM, and Sunday morning at 9:30 AM, along with a dedicated Children's Worship Service on Sunday at 12:00 PM. The chapel provides a supportive environment for community members to connect, practice their faith, and participate in spiritual activities.

As an active centre for faith and fellowship, Iglesia Ni Cristo in Adelaide plays a vital role in preserving cultural ties and fostering mutual support within the local community.

name: Isabella Ellares - Cross Road Dental
description:
Isabella Ellares (also known as Izi) is a professional bilingual Filipino-Australian Dental Hygienist practising at Cross Road Dental in South Plympton, Adelaide. Starting her career in the dental industry in 2016 as a dental nurse, she went on to complete an Advanced Diploma of Oral Health at TAFE SA in 2021, receiving the prestigious Oral B Award for Outstanding Performance in Preventative Oral Care.

Patients frequently praise Isabella for her gentle, thorough approach and dedicated patient care, noting her ability to help people get their oral health back on track. Working alongside the experienced team at Cross Road Dental, she focuses on comprehensive oral health education, routine cleans, and preventative care for patients of all ages.

Fluent in both English and Tagalog (Filipino), Isabella helps native and Tagalog-speaking patients feel comfortable and well-informed throughout their visits. Cross Road Dental is conveniently located on Cross Road in South Plympton, offering a welcoming atmosphere for families and individuals seeking quality dental care.

name: J&N Pinoy and Asian Goods
description:
J&N Pinoy and Asian Goods is a welcoming Filipino grocery store conveniently located at Shop 4/22 Anderson Walk in Smithfield, right by the train station. Owned and operated by Jen, who is widely appreciated for her warm hospitality, the shop serves as a beloved community hub for local residents and visitors across Adelaide.

The store features a broad range of authentic Filipino ingredients, pantry staples, snacks, and sweets. Customers particularly praise the special creamy halo-halo and mais con yelo, which provide a refreshing treat during South Australia's warm months. The premises are clean and well organised, making it easy to browse through imported goods, cooking essentials, and favourite Filipino comfort foods.

In addition to in-store shopping, J&N Pinoy and Asian Goods offers local delivery services to support customers who are unwell or unable to travel. With reliable service, competitive prices, and a friendly atmosphere, this store brings a comforting taste of home to the Northern Adelaide community.

name: JNJ Panaderya
description: JNJ Panaderya is a cherished traditional Filipino bakery located on Beafield Road in Para Hills West, Adelaide. Widely celebrated across South Australia, it has earned a glowing reputation for crafting some of the finest freshly baked pandesal in the country. Local patrons and visitors alike flock to this welcoming panaderya for its authentic selection of traditional Filipino breads and sweet treats. Highlights of the menu include warm pandesal, soft Spanish bread, rich ensaymada, and refreshing Halo-Halo desserts, alongside savory snacks such as crispy pork crackling. Customers frequently highlight the warm hospitality, friendly staff, and generous portion sizes that capture the nostalgic taste of home. Whether you are looking for everyday table bread or a delicious Filipino dessert, JNJ Panaderya offers an authentic taste of the Philippines right in the northern suburbs of Adelaide. Note that the bakery operates from Tuesday to Saturday, making it a favorite morning stop for locals.

name: JNR Forwarders
description:
JNR Forwarders is a trusted cargo and freight forwarding service based in Salisbury Downs, South Australia. Conveniently located on Kings Road, the business specialises in reliable door-to-door sea freight and Balikbayan box shipments from Adelaide directly to destination addresses across the Philippines.

As an official partner agent for LBC Express and an accredited sea freight forwarder recognised by the Philippine Department of Trade and Industry (DTI), JNR Forwarders provides the local Filipino-Australian community with safe and efficient logistics solutions. Families and individuals rely on their service to send care packages, personal belongings, and household items back home to loved ones with complete peace of mind.

Whether you are packing your first Balikbayan box or scheduling regular cargo shipments, JNR Forwarders offers dependable assistance tailored to the needs of South Australia's vibrant Pinoy community.

name: Jim's Cleaning Northfield
description:
Jim's Cleaning Northfield is a trusted residential and commercial cleaning service based in Northfield, Adelaide. Owned and operated by Pauline and Ronwaldo, a dedicated Filipino couple with professional backgrounds as an associate nurse unit manager and disability support worker, the business provides tailored cleaning solutions for homes and offices across Adelaide's northern suburbs.

Their comprehensive service range includes regular domestic house cleaning, commercial office maintenance, vacate and end-of-lease bond cleaning, specialised carpet care, window washing, and detailed oven cleaning. Customers consistently praise Pauline and Ronwaldo for their friendly, courteous approach, prompt communication, and thorough attention to detail, with every service backed by the official Jim's Work Guarantee.

Beyond their daily business operations, Pauline and Ronwaldo are deeply engaged in the local Adelaide Filipino community. They actively volunteer with their local church fellowship and assist newly arrived Filipino migrants with temporary accommodation and community orientation. Whether you require a one-off deep clean before moving out or ongoing office maintenance, Jim's Cleaning Northfield delivers dependable, high-quality care tailored to your exact needs.

name: Jk Asian Grocery & Butcher
description:
JK Asian Grocery & Butcher is a convenient local Asian grocery store and butcher shop located inside Springbank Plaza Shopping Centre on Waterloo Corner Road in Paralowie, Adelaide. Serving the local community in Adelaide's northern suburbs, the store offers a broad selection of authentic Asian ingredients, dry goods, pantry staples, and fresh produce.

The store features a dedicated butchery section with quality fresh meats alongside a variety of fresh Asian greens, fruits, vegetables, and everyday pantry items. Shoppers can also find popular Filipino groceries, traditional sauces, and specialty ingredients for home-cooked meals.

Regular customers frequently praise the attentive, friendly customer service from the store owner and staff, who are always happy to answer questions, share product recommendations, and assist shoppers with heavy boxes. Conveniently situated in Paralowie with easy shopping centre parking, JK Asian Grocery & Butcher provides a reliable local destination for fresh meats, produce, and Asian culinary favourites.

name: JnJ FILIPINO BAKERY
description:
JNJ Panaderya (formerly JnJ Filipino Bakery) is a cherished Pinoy bakery located at 21 Beafield Road in Para Hills West, Adelaide. Known throughout South Australia for bringing authentic Philippine baking traditions to the local community, this welcoming bakery offers a wide range of freshly baked breads, savoury treats, and sweet delights.

Local patrons praise JNJ Panaderya for its daily baked pandesal, which is soft, warm, and reminiscent of neighbourhood panaderias back home. Beyond classic pandesal, the bakery features popular specialties such as sweet Spanish bread, traditional halo-halo, and custom celebration cakes. Visitors consistently highlight the friendly service, generous portions, and authentic flavours that make every visit feel like a taste of home.

Whether you are dropping in for morning pastries or picking up takeaway treats for a family gathering, JNJ Panaderya provides a warm gathering spot for the Filipino-Australian community. The bakery operates from Tuesday through Saturday, offering convenient takeaway service in northern Adelaide.

name: Jonels Auto Mechanical Repair & Maintanance Shop
description:
Jonels Auto Mechanical Repair & Maintanance Shop is a trusted Filipino-owned automotive workshop located in Wingfield, South Australia. Run by experienced mechanic Jonel alongside his wife Mary, this family-operated business has served local motorists across the Adelaide metropolitan region for nearly thirty years with dedication and integrity.

Customers frequently praise the team for their transparent explanations, exceptional technical skills, and fair, competitive pricing. Whether performing routine vehicle servicing, logbook maintenance, tyre rotations, or custom mechanical upgrades—such as fitting lift kits and protective bash plates—Jonel takes genuine care of every vehicle brought into the workshop. The business is widely recognised for its welcoming atmosphere, honest advice, and willingness to assist both long-time clients and new vehicle owners.

As a valued mechanical service provider in Wingfield, Jonels Auto Mechanical Repair & Maintanance Shop continues to build strong relationships within the Filipino-Australian community and among drivers across South Australia looking for dependable automotive repairs.

name: Juan Kusinero
description:
Juan Kusinero was a beloved Filipino restaurant and takeaway situated along Payneham Road in Royston Park, Adelaide. The venue offered authentic, home-style Filipino meals in a casual and friendly dining space reminiscent of traditional carinderias in the Philippines.

Locals and visitors frequently praised signature dishes such as sizzling pork sisig, crunchy lechon kawali, crispy pata, and savoury longanisa entrees. Patrons appreciated the rich, well-balanced flavours, generous portion sizes, and welcoming service provided by the owners and kitchen staff. The menu featured a wide array of classic Filipino specialities, pork and seafood favourites, and takeaway options tailored for families and group gatherings.

The eatery served as a popular neighbourhood destination for authentic Southeast Asian flavours, bringing together community members and food enthusiasts from all across metropolitan Adelaide. While it was widely appreciated for its authentic hospitality and classic Pinoy recipes, this business has permanently closed.

name: Kitchener's Bakeshop
description:
Kitchener’s Bakeshop is a vibrant bakery located in Pooraka, South Australia, specialising in freshly baked breads, pastries, and traditional Filipino delicacies. Situated on Desmond Avenue, this welcoming local business has quickly become a favourite gathering spot for both the local neighbourhood and the broader Adelaide Filipino community.

Patrons consistently highlight the bakeshop’s authentic Filipino specialities, including warm ube cheese pandesal, rich yema flan cakes, biscoff cakes, and refreshing ube halo-halo stacked with colourful shaved ice layers. The bakery also serves iconic South Australian favourites such as classic Kitchener buns, savoury sausage rolls, cheese rolls, and flaky morning pastries, alongside expertly brewed coffee for dine-in or takeaway.

In addition to daily baked treats, Kitchener’s Bakeshop creates custom decorated cakes tailored for birthdays, family milestones, and special events. With warm customer service, accessible store hours from Wednesday to Sunday, and convenient online ordering options, the bakeshop offers a delightful blend of comforting Australian and Filipino flavours.

name: Kwento at Rekado Filipino Food Catering
description:
Kwento at Rekado is a popular family-owned Filipino catering service based in Paralowie, Adelaide, bringing authentic Pinoy comfort food to local celebrations and milestone events. Specialising in vibrant party trays and traditional home-style dishes, they are widely praised by the local community for delivering authentic flavours that remind customers of home.

Their menu features traditional Filipino favourites, including rich Kare-Kare, savoury Pastil, and a diverse range of classic Pinoy specialities tailored for gatherings of any size. Beyond food preparation, Kwento at Rekado offers chafing dish rentals to ensure catered events run seamlessly, with convenient local pick-up and delivery options available across metropolitan Adelaide.

Whether you are hosting a baby's first birthday, a christening, or a family reunion, Kwento at Rekado provides reliable, crowd-pleasing catering that makes every event special. Customers can easily arrange bookings and custom orders via their active social channels.

name: L&R Smallgoods Adelaide
description:
L&R Smallgoods Adelaide is a dedicated Filipino smallgoods manufacturer and retailer based in Prospect, South Australia. Specialising in authentic, hand-made Filipino meats, the business is best known across Adelaide for its signature Chorizo de Cebu and sweet and spicy longganisa. Every batch is crafted using traditional recipes and quality Australian ingredients, delivering rich, nostalgic flavours that resonate with the local Filipino-Australian community.

Customers and community members consistently praise the authentic taste of their smallgoods, particularly highlighting the savoury depth and spice of their Cebu-style chorizo. Beyond direct community sales from their Prospect shopfront, L&R Smallgoods supplies major Asian and Filipino grocery supermarkets throughout South Australia, including popular stockists such as Driver Supermarket. Whether you are preparing a traditional Pinoy breakfast at home or sourcing authentic Filipino sausage for a family gathering, L&R Smallgoods provides an essential taste of home right in Adelaide.

name: LBC
description:
LBC in Port Adelaide provides reliable cargo forwarding and balikbayan box services for the Filipino-Australian community across South Australia. Operating as an established local depot for LBC Express, the location specialises in sea freight shipping and air cargo solutions, helping local families send personal gifts, care packages, and essential goods home to the Philippines. Customers can purchase empty shipping boxes directly at the depot or schedule convenient door-to-door parcel pickups.

Local clients frequently praise the warm and accommodating customer service, noting how simple and straightforward it is to organise international shipments. The friendly staff assist customers through every step of the packing and declaration process, making sure each box is sealed and handled securely before transit.

Located on College Street in Port Adelaide, the depot operates during scheduled afternoon and weekend hours. Whether you are sending care packages or arranging regular freight, LBC serves as a trusted logistics link connecting South Australian residents with their loved ones across the Philippine archipelago.

name: Lechon Republic
description:
Lechon Republic was a highly popular Filipino restaurant situated on Prospect Road in Prospect, Adelaide. Run by a pair of welcoming brothers, the cozy diner specialised in authentic charcoal-grilled dishes and traditional Filipino comfort food.

Diners in Adelaide celebrated the establishment for its standout pork lechon, featuring perfectly crispy skin and tender, succulent meat. Popular menu highlights included rich lechon kare-kare with peanuty gravy, savoury pork sisig, charcoal-grilled chicken inasal, and flavourful bagoong (shrimp paste). Guests also enjoyed unique specialty offerings such as marinated crocodile spare ribs and charcoal-baked seafood sinigang. For dessert, the restaurant served beloved classics including halo-halo topped with leche flan and maja blanca.

Known for its vibrant rotisserie display and friendly, attentive service, Lechon Republic built a dedicated following among locals and the wider Filipino community across South Australia.

This business has permanently closed.

name: Lip Bites by Jego
description:
Lip Bites by Jego is a boutique meal preparation and catering service based in Melrose Park, Adelaide. The business specialises in preparing high-quality, home-style dishes with an ever-changing weekly menu that keeps meal times exciting and diverse.

Local patrons warmly highlight the generous portion sizes, rich flavours, and exceptional customer care provided by the team. A signature element of their operations is a strong commitment to environmental sustainability, utilising eco-friendly glass and reusable containers that are collected and sanitised every week to eliminate single-use packaging waste.

Operating directly out of Melrose Park, the service offers seamless weekday deliveries that provide peace of mind for busy families and individuals seeking wholesome, ready-to-enjoy food. Personalised updates keep customers informed about delivery schedules, ensuring a smooth experience from order to doorstep. From weekly family dinner solutions to tailored catering for special gatherings, Lip Bites by Jego combines culinary passion with reliable community-focused service across Adelaide.

name: Local Congregation of Port Adelaide
description:
Local Congregation of Port Adelaide is a vibrant Christian church community serving Filipino-Australians across Port Adelaide, Woodville Gardens, Alberton, and the surrounding western suburbs of Adelaide. As a local congregation of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church of Christ) operating within the Ecclesiastical District of Victoria, the church provides an encouraging space for worship, spiritual growth, and cultural fellowship.

The congregation hosts regular English-language worship services on Thursday evenings at 7:30 PM and Saturday afternoons at 2:30 PM, alongside Children's Worship Services (CWS) on Saturdays at 5:00 PM. These gatherings centre on biblical teachings, community prayer, and fostering strong spiritual bonds among families and individuals.

Situated at 234 Port Road in Alberton, the church venue features wheelchair-accessible entrances and on-site parking to accommodate all members of the congregation. Whether you are looking for a regular place of worship or seeking to connect with the local Filipino Christian community in South Australia, the Port Adelaide congregation extends a warm welcome to all.

name: Loreto Pinoy Store
description:
Loreto Pinoy Store is a welcoming Filipino grocery shop and convenience store situated on Oaklands Road in Marion, Adelaide. Founded as a true home away from home for the local community, the store is widely cherished for its warm hospitality, clean surroundings, and friendly owners.

Shoppers can explore an extensive range of authentic Filipino pantry essentials, imported snacks, canned goods, frozen favourites, and fresh seafood including tilapia and bangus. Beyond everyday groceries, Loreto Pinoy Store is famous for its delicious freshly prepared hot meals and traditional Pinoy delicacies. Popular treats include batchoy, savoury cheese rolls, homemade cassava cake, creamy leche flan, and refreshing halo-halo dessert.

Whether you are craving nostalgic childhood snacks or stocking up on quality ingredients for family meals, Loreto Pinoy Store provides a friendly one-stop shop experience in Marion.

name: Loreto's Kitchen
description:
Loreto's Kitchen is a beloved Filipino restaurant located in Findon, Adelaide, offering authentic Pinoy comfort food and home-style cooking. The eatery is well known among locals and the Filipino-Australian community for serving generous portions of traditional favourites at affordable prices.

Diners consistently praise signature offerings such as tender Chicken Inasal, mouthwatering Bihon Guisado, and rich Crispy Pork Kare-Kare. Their La Paz Batchoy is widely celebrated as one of the best in town, featuring a deeply savoury, flavourful broth. For dessert, visitors enjoy popular treats including Halo-Halo topped with sweet delicacies. The relaxed atmosphere, friendly service, and welcoming vibe make it a favourite spot for family meals and casual dining.

Note: This venue is temporarily closed for maintenance and repair works, with plans to reopen to the community soon.

name: Manila
description:
Manila is a beloved Filipino eatery situated on Semaphore Road in the coastal suburb of Semaphore, Adelaide. Celebrated for bringing vibrant Southeast Asian street food culture to South Australia, the restaurant offered locals and visitors an authentic taste of traditional Filipino hospitality and cuisine.

The menu featured popular street food favourites and hearty comfort dishes. Guests frequently raved about the signature Dabarkads Platter—a generous sharing feast laden with pork skewers, fishballs, crispy calamares, kwekwek, and crunchy okoy fritters. Other standout offerings included golden Pritong Lechon (crispy roast pork belly), fragrant Pancit Palabok with rich seafood gravy, and grilled Chicken Inasal paired with a unique tangy sinigang gravy. For dessert, patrons delighted in colourful cups of classic Halo-Halo topped with sweet jellies, purple yam, and ice cream.

With its warm, beachside dining atmosphere and friendly service, Manila provided a memorable community gathering spot for casual meals, family celebrations, and live music nights. Please note that this business is currently listed as temporarily closed.

name: Manila Trading Adelaide
description:
Manila Trading Adelaide was a cherished local Filipino grocery store and community hub situated in Station Arcade on Hindley Street in the heart of Adelaide CBD. For many years, the shop served as an essential one-stop hub for the South Australian Filipino community.

Local patrons warmly remembered the store for its friendly, accommodating staff and its extensive selection of authentic Pinoy pantry staples. Customers regularly visited to stock up on canned goods, snacks, tuyo, dilis, and popular desserts like ube and langka ice cream. Beyond retail groceries, the store offered fresh Filipino baked goods, including ensaymada, pandesal, and bibingka, alongside a popular Saturday breakfast service.

In addition to food offerings, Manila Trading provided vital community services under one roof. Patrons relied on the store to send money remittances to family in the Philippines and send parcels back home via balikbayan boxes.

Note: Manila Trading Adelaide on Hindley Street has permanently closed.

name: Manoora Filipino & Asian Grocery
description:
Manoora Filipino & Asian Grocery is a welcoming neighbourhood Asian supermarket located on Manoora Street in Greenacres, Adelaide. Operating under new management, the store provides the local community with an extensive selection of authentic Filipino ingredients, imported snacks, and essential household staples.

Shoppers frequently praise the friendly, family-orientated service and helpful staff who assist customers with finding hard-to-source products. The grocer features a wide range of popular Filipino pantry goods, including frozen delicacies, traditional ice creams like ube, and specialty ingredients alongside fresh daily essentials such as milk, bread, cold drinks, and fresh produce. The store also offers freshly baked breads and treats, making it a favourite spot for locals seeking authentic home flavours.

Conveniently situated in Greenacres with wheelchair-accessible entrance facilities and on-site money transfer services via Ria Money Transfer, Manoora Filipino & Asian Grocery is open Tuesday through Saturday from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM, serving as a vibrant hub for the local Filipino-Australian community.

name: Mater Christi Parish Church
description:
Mater Christi Parish Church, located on Grange Road in Seaton, is a welcoming Catholic community in Adelaide's western suburbs. Serving a diverse congregation, the parish provides a supportive environment for parishioners to gather, nurture their spiritual life, and connect with fellow community members across various cultural backgrounds.

Parishioners frequently highlight the warm and inclusive atmosphere of the church, praising the dedicated pastoral team and active group of local volunteers.
